formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's the complete formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Mathematics - Conic Sections in JSON format:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Standard form of a Circle",
    "formula": "(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2",
    "variables": "h = x-coordinate of the center, k = y-coordinate of the center, r = radius",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use to find the equation of a circle given its center and radius."
  },
  {
    "name": "Equation of a Circle in general form",
    "formula": "x^2 + y^2 + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0",
    "variables": "g = x-coordinate of the center, f = y-coordinate of the center, c = (g^2 + f^2 - r^2)",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use to find the equation of a circle given its general form."
  },
  {
    "name": "Equation of an Ellipse in standard form",
    "formula": "((x - h)^2)/a^2 + ((y - k)^2)/b^2 = 1",
    "variables": "h = x-coordinate of the center, k = y-coordinate of the center, a = semi-major axis, b = semi-minor axis",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use to find the equation of an ellipse given its center and axes."
  },
  {
    "name": "Equation of an Ellipse in general form",
    "formula": "(a^2x^2 + b^2y^2)/c^2 + 2gx + 2fy + d = 0",
    "variables": "a = semi-major axis, b = semi-minor axis, c = distance between foci, g = x-coordinate of the center, f = y-coordinate of the center, d = (c^2 - a^2 - b^2)",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use to find the equation of an ellipse given its general form."
  },
  {
    "name": "Equation of a Hyperbola in standard form",
    "formula": "((x - h)^2)/a^2 - ((y - k)^2)/b^2 = 1",
    "variables": "h = x-coordinate of the center, k = y-coordinate of the center, a = distance between vertices, b = distance between co-vertices",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use to find the equation of a hyperbola given its center and axes."
  },
  {
    "name": "Equation of a Hyperbola in general form",
    "formula": "(a^2x^2 - b^2y^2)/c^2 - 2gx - 2fy + d = 0",
    "variables": "a = distance between vertices, b = distance between co-vertices, c = distance between foci, g = x-coordinate of the center, f = y-coordinate of the center, d = (c^2 - a^2 + b^2)",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use to find the equation of a hyperbola given its general form."
  },
  {
    "name": "Equation of a Parabola in standard form",
    "formula": "x^2 = 4ay or y^2 = 4ax",
    "variables": "a = distance between focus and vertex",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use to find the equation of a parabola given its focus and vertex."
  },
  {
    "name": "Equation of a Parabola in general form",
    "formula": "y^2 = 4ax + 2bx + c or x^2 = 4ay + 2by + c",
    "variables": "a = distance between focus and vertex, b = x or y coordinate of the focus, c = constant",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use to find the equation of a parabola given its general form."
  },
  {
    "name": "Eccentricity of a Conic",
    "formula": "e = c/a",
    "variables": "c = distance between foci, a = distance between vertices",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use to find the eccentricity of a conic given its foci and vertices."
  },
  {
    "name": "Length of the latus rectum of a Conic",
    "formula": "2b^2/a",
    "variables": "a = distance between vertices, b = distance between co-vertices",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use to find the length of the latus rectum of a conic given its vertices and co-vertices."
  },
  {
    "name": "Focal Distance of a Conic",
    "formula": "p = a^2/b",
    "variables": "a = distance between vertices, b = distance between co-vertices",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use to find the focal distance of a conic given its vertices and co-vertices."
  },
  {
    "name": "Directrix of a Parabola",
    "formula": "x = -a or y = -a",
    "variables": "a = distance between focus and vertex",
    "units": "None",
    "notes": "Use
